description: This figure presents TEM characterization of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs). Panel (a) is a bright-field TEM image of the as-hot-rolled S0 sample showing numerous κ-carbides (5–10 nm in diameter, indicated by yellow arrows) irregularly distributed within the austenitic matrix. Panel (a1) provides the corresponding HRTEM lattice image and FFT pattern indexed along the [110] zone axis, confirming the crystalline nature of the κ-carbide precipitates. Panel (a2) shows the high-density, irregularly distributed dislocations in S0 and an SAED pattern of the austenite matrix along [110]. Panel (b) displays the twin morphology after solution treatment, with SAED patterns revealing both matrix and twin reflections along [110], indicating abundant annealing twin boundaries (ATBs). The figure demonstrates that the as-rolled microstructure contains nanoscale κ-carbides and high dislocation density, whereas solution treatment reduces dislocation density and introduces ATBs that effectively hinder dislocation movement, supporting the grain size optimization strategy for enhancing corrosion resistance through Al-rich oxide film formation.
